debug_info.goal:
r : ℝ
hr : r ≥ 0
⊢ HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1))

verification_message: All 1 attempts failed (1 strategies x 1 attempts each)
proof_attempts:
have h₁ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
  have h₂ : MeasurableSet (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := measurableSet_Icc
  have h₃ : (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) ⊆ Set.univ := by
    intro x hx
    trivial
  -- Since Φ₄ r is continuous and the interval [0,1] is compact, Φ₄ r is bounded and thus has a finite integral.
  have h₄ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    -- Use the fact that continuous functions on compact sets are bounded and the integral of a bounded function over a bounded domain is finite.
    apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral
    · exact h₁
    · exact h₂
  -- The result follows from the fact that the integral is finite.
  exact h₄
